name: Zhou Yu
description: Zhou Yu (175–210 AD), courtesy name Gongjin, was a prominent military general and strategist serving under the warlord Sun Ce during the late Eastern Han dynasty. He continued his service under Sun Ce's younger brother Sun Quan after the former's death. Zhou Yu is best known for his pivotal role in the Battle of Red Cliffs in 208 AD, where he commanded the allied forces of Sun Quan and Liu Bei and decisively defeated the numerically superior forces of Cao Cao, preventing Cao Cao from conquering the south and unifying China. He was renowned for his strategic brilliance, military prowess, and refined demeanor, excelling in both military affairs and the arts. His handsome appearance and musical talent further enhanced his reputation. Despite his relatively short life, Zhou Yu left an indelible mark on Chinese history and is celebrated as one of the greatest strategists of the Three Kingdoms period. He is often romanticized in literature and popular culture, most notably in the historical novel Romance of the Three Kingdoms, solidifying his legendary status. His rivalry with Zhuge Liang, while often exaggerated in fiction, remains a captivating aspect of his legacy. Zhou Yu's contributions were crucial in establishing and securing the foundation of the state of Eastern Wu.
subtitle: Brilliant strategist of the Three Kingdoms
achievements: Victory at the Battle of Red Cliffs
Known for his handsome appearance and musical talent

keyMyths.title: The Battle of Red Cliffs
keyMyths.description: Zhou Yu orchestrated the stunning defeat of Cao Cao's massive army at Red Cliffs. His strategic brilliance and use of fire ships turned the tide of the war. This victory prevented Cao Cao from unifying China.

keyMyths.title: Zhou Yu's rivalry with Zhuge Liang
keyMyths.description: Zhou Yu, envious of Zhuge Liang's wisdom and abilities, repeatedly attempted to undermine or eliminate him. These attempts were often thwarted by Zhuge Liang's superior intellect. This rivalry is heavily romanticized in fiction.

keyMyths.title: Zhou Yu's Musical Talent
keyMyths.description: Zhou Yu was known for his keen ear for music. It was said that he could identify any mistake in a musical performance, earning him the saying: 'A mistake in music, Zhou Yu will know'.
